For the past three or four years
the morning paper of this city has
studiously endeavored to make the
people of this community believe
that those in charge of the govern
ment of Spokane have been guilty
of misuse of public funds. extrava


gance and dishonesty. Many right
eous citizens, believing that no
paper would publish accusations of
this character without foundation,
were lead to believe that this was
the case. 1t succeeded finally in
arousing such sentiment that
a grand jury was called by the SUI
perior court of this county for the
purpose of investigating the CON
duct of city and county affairs.
That grand jury convened July IS,
1905, pursuant to the call. At that
time Mayor Daggett had been in
Office about two months, and, with
the exception of those two months
had been city comptroller of the
city OF Spokane for the preceding
four years, and as such. in accord
ance with the city charter, he~exer-

bert Moore was foreman of that

ing Information in regard to any

ined under oath. Upon completing
